example_sentences: If the client does not send an ACK to complete the third step of this 3-way hand- shake, eventually (often after a minute or more) the server will terminate the half-open connection and reclaim the allocated resources.

The very ( very ) observant reader will recall that the three-way TCP handshake protocol needed to address the same problem—the server side of a TCP connection did not want to accept a connection if the received SYN segment was an old copy (retransmission) of a SYN segment from an earlier connection.

example_sentences: Within an end system, a transport protocol moves messages from application processes to the network edge (that is, the network layer) and vice versa, but it doesn’t have any say about how the messages are moved within the network core.
